title: Dinner — Firehouse Brewing Company
description: 🍺 South Dakota's oldest craft brewery, set inside a beautifully restored 1915 brick firehouse. Great pub food — burgers, ribs, and hand-cut steaks — alongside award-winning house brews. Lively atmosphere and right in downtown Rapid City.

title: 🧺 Picnic Lunch — Cooke City Rest Stop
description: 🥪 Pack and eat lunch at a scenic pullout or picnic area in Cooke City, just before the Northeast Entrance to Yellowstone. A rustic mountain town with great views of the Beartooth Plateau. Stretch your legs before the final push into the park.

details.Tip: Pick up any last supplies at Cooke City General Store

title: Dinner — Wonderland Café
description: 🌿 A beloved local gem in Gardiner with an eclectic, farm-to-table menu featuring organic ingredients, locally-sourced meats, and an excellent selection of Montana craft beers. Warm, cozy atmosphere and one of the best meals you'll find near the North Entrance.

title: 🧺 Picnic Lunch — Grand Canyon Rim
location: Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone — Rim Overlooks
description: 🥪 Eat lunch at one of the rim overlooks near Artist Point or Lookout Point with a breathtaking view of the 308-ft Lower Falls plunging into the brilliant yellow-orange canyon. One of the most dramatic picnic backdrops in all of North America.

details.Picnic Spot: Artist Point or Lookout Point Overlook
details.Tip: Arrive early — overlooks get crowded by midday

title: Dinner — Wild West Pizzeria & Saloon
description: 🍕 A rustic and raucous West Yellowstone staple. Hand-tossed pizzas with creative wild game toppings (elk, bison), craft beer on tap, and a classic western saloon atmosphere. A fun end to a day exploring the park's thermal wonders.

title: 🧺 Picnic Lunch — Firehole River
location: Firehole River — Midway Geyser Basin Area
description: 🥪 Pull over for a picnic lunch along the banks of the Firehole River near the Midway Geyser Basin. Listen to the rushing thermal river, watch bison graze in the adjacent meadows, and breathe in the wild Yellowstone air. A magical spot with steam rising in the background.

details.Tip: Check bison proximity — keep a safe 25-yard distance

title: Dinner — The Pioneer Grill
location: The Pioneer Grill — Jackson Lake Lodge
address: 101 Jackson Lake Lodge Rd, Moran, WY 83013
description: 🍽️ A beloved 1950s-style diner inside the iconic Jackson Lake Lodge, featuring a sweeping 70-foot counter where you can watch the Teton Range light up at dusk. Classic American comfort food — trout, burgers, and homemade pie. No reservations needed; just walk in and grab a stool.

title: 🧺 Picnic Lunch — Bradley Lake Shoreline
location: Bradley Lake — Grand Teton National Park
description: 🥪 Enjoy a trail lunch on the rocky shores of Bradley Lake with a direct, jaw-dropping view straight up at the Grand Teton. The lake's still surface perfectly mirrors the towering peaks. One of the most scenic lunch spots on the trip.

details.Tip: Carry out all trash — no bins on the trail

title: Dinner — Trapper Grill
description: 🏔️ A casual lakeside restaurant perched right on Jackson Lake at Signal Mountain Lodge. Famous for their loaded nachos, elk burgers, local trout, and a full bar with stunning Teton views. Great atmosphere for families after a big hiking day.

details.Known For: Giant Nachos, Elk Burgers & Lake Views

title: 🧺 Picnic Lunch — Cascade Canyon
location: Cascade Canyon — Grand Teton National Park
description: 🥪 Find a flat boulder along the Cascade Canyon creek and eat lunch deep inside the most iconic canyon in the Tetons. Sheer granite walls tower 3,000 ft on either side. Keep an eye out for moose in the willows as you eat — sightings are very common here.

details.Tip: About 2.5 miles in from the Jenny Lake boat landing

title: Dinner — The Mural Room
location: The Mural Room — Jackson Lake Lodge
address: 101 Jackson Lake Lodge Rd, Moran, WY 83013
description: ✨ The crown jewel dining experience of the trip. The Mural Room is a fine-dining institution at Jackson Lake Lodge, featuring floor-to-ceiling 60-foot windows perfectly framing the Grand Teton and Jackson Lake at sunset. Regional cuisine — Snake River trout, Wyoming elk, wild game — executed with elegance. Reservations are essential.

title: Lunch — Nora's Fish Creek Inn
description: 🏆 A James Beard Award-winning breakfast and lunch landmark in the tiny mountain town of Wilson. Famous for massive, golden waffles, trout, and eggs in a warm rustic cabin setting. A true Jackson Hole institution — lines can form early, but it's worth every minute.

title: Dinner — Dornan's Spur Bar & Pizzeria
description: 🍕 A legendary Jackson Hole gathering spot right inside the park at Moose Village. Order wood-fired pizzas, cold beers, and then head up to the rooftop deck for one of the most spectacular views of the Grand Teton you'll find anywhere. The perfect casual celebration dinner after an epic hiking week.

title: Lunch — The Middle Fork
description: 🌊 A fresh, seasonal café in the charming town of Lander at the foot of the Wind River Range. Light, riverfront dining with creative sandwiches, salads, and local ingredients. A wonderful midway stop on the long drive back south toward Cheyenne.

title: Dinner — The Albany Restaurant
description: 🍽️ A Cheyenne institution operating since 1942, just steps from the historic Union Pacific depot. Classic American comfort food — steaks, burgers, chicken-fried steak — in a warm, no-frills setting that locals have loved for generations. The perfect place to wind down after a long final driving day.

title: 🧺 Roadside Diner Lunch
description: 🥪 Quick highway lunch stop on the final stretch home. Find a local diner or fast-casual spot along the route to refuel everyone for the last leg. A great time to share trip highlights and favorite memories before pulling into the driveway.

details.Tip: Check Google Maps for top-rated spots along your exact route home
